name: Astro component composition pattern
evidence: Uses BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner as composable section components — consistent pattern likely repo-wide for Saltgrass Modular pages

name: PUBLIC_API_URL + PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID env pattern
evidence: Both vars referenced at top of file as Astro env imports with Railway fallback URL — consistent pattern across all saltgrass-modular API routes

name: Silent-fail / empty-response on error
evidence: AbortError caught and returns degraded status rather than throwing — same silent-fail convention seen in s.ts and t.ts

name: Same-origin proxy for ad-blocker bypass
evidence: Rewrites tracking URL from absolute backend URL to /api/t — repo-wide pattern for LPAI client sites to bypass ad-blocker domain blacklists

name: BlogPosting JSON-LD schema injection
evidence: Inline <script type='application/ld+json'> with BlogPosting schema including headline, author, publisher, datePublished, wordCount — likely a repo-wide pattern for blog detail pages

name: astro:page-load event listener pattern
evidence: Client-side scripts initialize on both DOMContentLoaded and astro:page-load to handle Astro view transitions

name: Build-time data-attribute injection for client-side filtering
evidence: data-cat / data-title / data-excerpt injected on each post card element, enabling zero-latency JS filtering without any client-side API calls

name: Honeypot bot-protection pattern on all forms
evidence: Both QuoteRequestForm and ConsultationForm include a visually-hidden input name='website_url' with aria-hidden and position:absolute;left:-9999px. The submit handler checks fd.get('website_url') and silently succeeds without posting if filled.

name: LPAI portal forms API as form submission target
evidence: Both forms POST to /api/portal/forms/<slug>/submit on the LPAI Railway backend with { locationId, data, meta } shape

name: BaseLayout accepts keywords prop for SEO
evidence: Line 11 passes keywords='modular development, developer solutions, multi-unit housing, volume production' to BaseLayout — first file in this batch to use the keywords prop, suggesting BaseLayout renders a <meta name=keywords> tag

name: Inline onclick modal trigger pattern
evidence: Line 299: onclick directly calls document.getElementById('consultation-modal')?.classList.remove('hidden') — same pattern as index.astro's CTA buttons

name: Native HTML details/summary accordion for FAQ
evidence: Lines 257-315 use <details class='...' group> with Tailwind group-open utilities rather than a JS-driven accordion — a progressive-enhancement pattern

name: data-action modal trigger pattern
evidence: Lines 87-90: querySelectorAll('[data-action="modalId"]') iterates all triggers and attaches click→classList.remove('hidden'). Allows any element anywhere in the page to open a named modal without inline JS.

name: Modal outside-click dismiss
evidence: Lines 94-98: listens on the modal backdrop div itself; closes when e.target.id === modalId, meaning clicks on the backdrop (not the inner content) close the modal.

name: Direct contact CTA pattern (phone + email) for specialty verticals
evidence: military.astro has a dedicated 'Ready to Deploy?' section with tel: and mailto: links to Dylan Walker rather than routing to /contact or a form embed — same as homeowners.astro likely does for its CTA

name: BaseLayout keywords prop usage is inconsistent across pages
evidence: military.astro passes keywords prop; container-homes.astro and pools.astro do not — no enforced contract at the component level

name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for product catalogs
evidence: models array defined in Astro frontmatter (lines 6-75) drives the entire page render — no fetch to LPAI backend for product data, unlike blog/[slug].astro which calls the API

name: Dead CTA button pattern
evidence: 'Request Quote' buttons exist in both container-homes.astro and pools.astro with no action wired — consistent across model catalog pages

name: Inline onclick + global window function for client-side interactivity
evidence: scrollToSection() defined in <script> block called via onclick attribute strings — bypasses Astro's module scoping, a pattern that works but is fragile

name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for process/stages content
evidence: stages array (lines 6-97) drives the entire timeline visualization and detail cards — no CMS fetch, same pattern as model catalog pages

name: Client-side DOM filter pattern via global onclick + data-category attributes
evidence: filterProjects() reads data-category from card elements and toggles display:none/block — same inline-onclick-global-function pattern as pools.astro scrollToSection()

name: Gradient string as image placeholder in data arrays
evidence: projects array stores Tailwind gradient class strings in the image field (e.g. 'gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600') used as bg- class suffix — signals real photos not yet integrated

name: SSR redirect via Astro.redirect / new Response
evidence: File uses `import { redirect } from 'astro'` and `new Response(..., { status: 404 })` — Astro SSR response-termination pattern

name: Astro static site generation with hardcoded slug manifest
evidence: getStaticPaths() returns a literal array of slug objects rather than fetching from backend — content-as-code pattern, unlike blog/[slug].astro which fetches LPAI backend

name: Inline data arrays for static content
evidence: specs, features, and faqItems defined as JS arrays in frontmatter and mapped in template — reusable without a CMS

name: Hardcoded contact info pattern
evidence: Phone and email embedded directly in template markup rather than pulled from a config or CMS — same pattern used across saltgrass-modular service pages

name: Three-import Astro page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
evidence: pools.astro imports exactly BaseLayout, Hero, CtaBanner and no other dependencies — same pattern seen in about.astro and traditional-builds.astro

name: Fully static service detail pages with no runtime data fetching
evidence: No fetch/API call anywhere in the file; all content is hardcoded in the template

name: Inline .map() for dynamic list rendering in Astro templates
evidence: Lines 65-77 (architectural styles), 107-125 (included items) use .map() with JSX-style rendering inside Astro templates

name: Nav/Footer slot injection pattern in web-marketing
evidence: terms.astro passes <Nav slot='nav'> and <Footer slot='footer'> to BaseLayout — distinct from saltgrass-modular which uses no explicit slots

name: IntersectionObserver for sticky sidebar TOC active-state
evidence: Client-side script (lines 317-341) uses IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-80px 0px -60% 0px' to highlight the TOC link matching the on-screen section

name: Mobile vs desktop TOC via accordion + sticky sidebar
evidence: Two parallel TOC implementations: mobile accordion (lines 41-58, JS toggle at 284-302) and desktop sticky sidebar (lines 65-79). Same sections array drives both.

name: Silent-fail / 204 empty response on backend error
evidence: s.ts, t.ts, and health.ts all catch errors silently and return either empty body or degraded status — explicit design choice to never surface backend errors to end users

name: Astro static site generation with LPAI backend as content source
evidence: blog/[slug].astro uses getStaticPaths() calling backend /api/blog/posts to enumerate slugs at build time — SSG pattern with LPAI as headless CMS

name: BaseLayout accepts keywords prop for SEO meta
evidence: developers.astro line 11 passes keywords prop to BaseLayout — extends the known BaseLayout prop surface beyond title/description

name: Modals scoped exclusively to index.astro DOM
evidence: Both quote-modal and consultation-modal divs are only rendered in index.astro; other pages reference them via onclick/data-action creating a cross-page dependency that silently fails on direct navigation

name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for product and process content
evidence: container-homes.astro, pools.astro, and process.astro all define their primary content as static JS arrays in the Astro frontmatter — no LPAI backend fetch for product/pricing/stage data

name: Dead CTA button pattern on model catalog pages
evidence: 'Request Quote' buttons in container-homes.astro (line 157) and pools.astro (lines 104, 163) have no onclick, href, or form action — consistent unfinished wiring across catalog pages

name: Gradient CSS class string as image placeholder in data arrays
evidence: projects.astro stores Tailwind gradient strings in the image field consumed as bg- class suffix — signals placeholder state, not a real asset reference pattern

name: SSR redirect via Astro redirect() / new Response()
evidence: qr/[code].astro uses Astro's redirect() for 302 and `new Response('...', { status: 404 })` to terminate SSR — Astro SSR response-termination pattern

name: Gradient placeholder blocks instead of real images
evidence: container-homes.astro uses `bg-gradient-to-br` divs for model gallery and project gallery instead of tags — unfinished placeholder pattern that may ship to production

name: Three-import Astro service page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
evidence: Both pools.astro and traditional-builds.astro import exactly these three components and no others — a repeating template for all saltgrass-modular service detail pages

name: Fully static service detail pages with hardcoded pricing
evidence: pools.astro and traditional-builds.astro contain no fetch/API calls; all product specs and pricing are hardcoded in the Astro template — any price change requires a code edit

name: Dead CTA buttons pattern on service pages
evidence: pools.astro lines 58/96 have <button> elements with no action; traditional-builds.astro CTA banner has no link target — consistent unfinished wiring across service pages

name: Nav/Footer slot injection pattern in web-marketing vs implicit in saltgrass-modular
evidence: terms.astro (web-marketing) uses explicit slot='nav' and slot='footer' on Nav/Footer components; saltgrass-modular pages use no slots — two different layout composition patterns across the two apps
